pr.title: refactor melee to use BUILD dir
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Refactored the root Makefile so generated artifacts are written under a target-specific build directory, build/ssbm.us.1.2, instead of beside source and assembly inputs. The PR introduced TARGET, BUILD_DIR, SRC_DIRS, ASM_DIRS, target-prefixed object paths, build-directory creation, and updated SHA1 verification to use ssbm.us.1.2.sha1 pointing at build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added TARGET := ssbm.us.1.2 and BUILD_DIR := build/$(TARGET) to make the build output target-version-aware.
Added SRC_DIRS := src src/sysdolphin and ASM_DIRS := asm to centralize source and assembly directory lists.
Changed DOL from main.dol to $(BUILD_DIR)/main.dol and MAP from ssbm.map to $(BUILD_DIR)/ssbm.map.
Changed O_FILES so object files are generated under $(BUILD_DIR), preserving source-relative paths such as build/ssbm.us.1.2/src/... and build/ssbm.us.1.2/asm/....
Added ALL_DIRS and a DUMMY != mkdir -p $(ALL_DIRS) assignment to ensure build output directories exist before compilation.
Updated assembly and C pattern rules from %.o: %.s and %.o: %.c to $(BUILD_DIR)/%.o: %.s and $(BUILD_DIR)/%.o: %.c.
Changed SHA1 verification from sha1sum -c ssbm.sha1 to sha1sum -c $(TARGET).sha1.
Deleted ssbm.sha1, which referenced main.dol at the repository root.
Added ssbm.us.1.2.sha1 with the same hash but pointing to build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol.
Added a Makefile debug helper target, print-%, to inspect make variables.
smart_moves: Using TARGET as part of BUILD_DIR made the build tree ready for target-version-specific outputs instead of hardcoding root-level artifact names.
Preserving source-relative object paths under BUILD_DIR avoids collisions between files with the same basename in different source or assembly directories.
Moving the SHA1 filename to $(TARGET).sha1 keeps verification coupled to the selected build target.
Creating build, target, source, and assembly subdirectories before compilation prevents missing-directory failures once object output is redirected.
The print-% Makefile helper is a lightweight debugging convention for inspecting variable expansion during build-system refactors.
decomp_lessons: Keep generated DOL, ELF, MAP, and object files out of source directories; a target-specific build directory makes cleanup and multi-version work easier.
When changing artifact locations, update verification files and expected paths at the same time or checksum validation will fail even if the binary still matches.
For decompilation projects with mirrored src/ and asm/ trees, object paths should preserve enough directory structure to avoid basename collisions.
Centralizing source and assembly directory lists makes later expansion of the project layout easier than scattering directory names through wildcard and mkdir logic.
A build refactor can be matching-neutral if hashes are preserved and only output paths change; here the SHA1 hash stayed the same while the checked path changed.
naming_conventions: TARGET uses the game/version-style name ssbm.us.1.2.
The target checksum file is named $(TARGET).sha1, here ssbm.us.1.2.sha1.
Generated main.dol is expected at $(BUILD_DIR)/main.dol rather than repository-root main.dol.
Makefile variables use uppercase names such as TARGET, BUILD_DIR, SRC_DIRS, ASM_DIRS, O_FILES, ALL_DIRS.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly source or matching logic changed directly in the provided diff.
The build rules still assemble .s files with $(AS) $(ASFLAGS) and compile .c files with $(CC) $(CFLAGS), then run $(PYTHON) $(POSTPROC) $(PROCFLAGS) on compiled C objects.
The SHA1 hash remained 08e0bf20134dfcb260699671004527b2d6bb1a45, indicating the refactor was intended to preserve the final main.dol output while changing its path.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that expand TARGET handling for other Melee versions or regions.
Find later Makefile changes that replace DUMMY != mkdir -p with order-only directory prerequisites or another directory creation strategy.
Search for later updates to clean behavior to ensure build/$(TARGET) artifacts and empty directories are removed consistently.
Look for PRs that add more SRC_DIRS or ASM_DIRS and reuse this target-prefixed object path pattern.
